title: Lumen — Phase 1 VRAM Benchmark Results
content:
# Phase 1 — VRAM Benchmark Results

## Hardware & Environment

- **GPU:** NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (8192 MB)
- **Driver:** 595.79
- **CUDA:** 12.6
- **Python:** 3.12.10
- **faster-whisper:** 1.1.1
- **Date:** 2026-03-11 03:20 UTC
- **Test audio:** C:\Dev\Lumen\test_audio\test_audio.mp3
- **Segment length:** 10s
- **Segments per model:** 10
- **Concurrent streams:** 3
- **Compute type:** float16
- **Beam size:** 5

## Benchmark Table

| Model | VRAM Idle | VRAM Peak (1 stream) | VRAM Peak (3 streams) | Latency avg | Latency p95 | Accuracy Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| base | 1897 MB | 2075 MB | 2075 MB | 233ms | 228ms | Music... |
| small | 2313 MB | 2715 MB | 2715 MB | 577ms | 345ms | MUSIC... |
| medium | 3625 MB | 3899 MB | 3899 MB | 430ms | 604ms | Thanks for watching!... |
| large-v3 | 5513 MB | 5851 MB | 5851 MB | 621ms | 877ms | 🎵... |

## Sample Transcriptions

### base

1. Music
2. . . . .
3. Welcome to the series premiere.

### small

1. MUSIC
2. You
3. Welcome to the series premiere.

### medium

1. Thanks for watching!
2. Thanks for watching!
3. Welcome to the series premiere.

### large-v3

1. 🎵
2. Thanks for watching!
3. Welcome to the series premiere

## Recommendation

**Recommended model: `medium`** (8 GB cards — RTX 3070, RTX 3080, RTX 4060 Ti, etc.)

- Peak VRAM 3899 MB leaves ~4.3 GB headroom for Foundry + browser + OS
- Latency avg 430ms / p95 604ms — comfortably under the 3-second target
- Good accuracy on spoken TTRPG dialogue; hallucinations on music/silence segments are a known Whisper behavior, mitigated by VAD

**Optimal model: `large-v3`** (12+ GB cards — RTX 3080 Ti, RTX 4070 Ti, etc.)

- Peak VRAM 5851 MB is tight on 8 GB but comfortable on 12 GB+
- Latency avg 621ms / p95 877ms — still well under the 3-second target
- Best accuracy and most natural transcription of all models tested

**Architecture decision: single shared Whisper instance with segment queue**

- VRAM peak is identical for 1 and 3 concurrent streams — CTranslate2 shares model weights across threads
- No benefit to loading separate per-stream model instances; single instance with a queue is simpler and uses less memory
- This means the pipeline can scale to 4-6 player streams without additional VRAM cost

**Not recommended: `base`**
- Fastest (233ms avg) but lower accuracy — truncates and drops content more than other models
- Only useful as a fallback for very low VRAM cards (< 6 GB)

**Not recommended as default: `small`**
- Reasonable accuracy but an unexplained latency outlier (3622ms) appeared during testing
- `medium` is only ~1.2 GB more VRAM and provides better accuracy with comparable latency
